history.text: Squamish (; ; 2021 census population 23,819) is a community and a district municipality in the Canadian province of British Columbia, located at the north end of Howe Sound on the.
The population of the Squamish census agglomeration is 24,232.
However, Western's operations in Squamish permanently ceased on January 26, 2006.
As well as over 300 climbing routes on the Chief proper, a majority of which require traditional climbing protection, there are steep hiking trails around the back to access the three.
In all, between Shannon Falls, Murrin Park, The Malamute, and the Smoke Bluffs, there are over 1500 rock-climbing routes in the Squamish area (and another 300 or so climbs north of.
In recent years, Squamish has also become a major destination for bouldering, with over 2500 problems described in the local guidebook.
